title: Air-fryer cauliflower steaks
subtitle: Cumin and smoked paprika cauliflower
excerpt: Thick cauliflower slices coated in a warm spice paste of cumin, smoked paprika, and olive oil, then cooked in the air fryer until charred at the edges and tender throughout. A substantial vegetable main or side. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Cauliflower steaks are thick cross-sections cut from the centre of the head, leaving the core intact to hold them together. The air fryer produces caramelisation at the cut edges that is difficult to replicate without very high oven heat. The cumin and smoked paprika combination is drawn from North African and Spanish cooking, where these spices are the standard pairing for brassicas and roasted vegetables.

content.text: Cutting a cauliflower into steaks gives each piece a flat cut surface that makes direct contact with the hot air and caramelises. The key is keeping the core intact: cut straight down through the centre so each slice holds together. The outer florets from the sides of the head can be tossed with the same spice paste and cooked alongside. They take a little less time.

items.name: Cauliflower
items.prepNote: outer leaves removed, cut into 2 cm steaks through the core

content.text: Mix together {{cumin-ground}} of ground cumin, {{paprika-smoked}} of smoked paprika, {{chilli-flakes}} of chilli flakes, {{olive-oil}} of olive oil, and {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt to make a paste. Brush or rub the paste over both cut surfaces of the cauliflower steaks. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C. Place the steaks in the basket in a single layer. Air-fry for 16 to 18 minutes, flipping carefully halfway through, until the edges are charred and the core is tender when pierced with a knife. Serve with yoghurt or tahini alongside.

items.fix: Cut straight down through the centre of the head with a long knife. Each steak needs a piece of the core, this is the binding. Steaks should be at least 2 cm thick.
items.cause: Core not kept intact when cutting, or steaks too thin.

items.fix: Reduce to 185°C and extend the time to 22 minutes. Check the core by inserting a skewer. It should meet no resistance.
items.cause: Temperature too high, or steak too thick.
items.symptom: Raw at the core, charred at the edges

items.fix: Cook in batches. Cauliflower gives off a lot of moisture, space is needed for it to escape.
items.symptom: Pale and steamed rather than charred

content.text: What to do with the offcuts

Two cauliflowers yield two or three centre steaks each; the rest breaks into florets. Toss the florets in the same spice paste, spread them in the basket, and air-fry at 200°C for 12 to 14 minutes until golden and catching at the edges. They make a good side dish for the steaks, or a topping for grain bowls.
